business: Build in Public - Fitness Business Journey
brand_voice: Transparent, authentic, data-driven founder sharing the journey
description: Behind-the-scenes content documenting the journey of building a personal training + peptide education business

revenue_updates.description: Weekly/monthly revenue, costs, and margins transparency

hooks: Made a ${amount} mistake today. Here's what happened:

3 things that didn't work this week:

Just automated {task}. Saves {time}/week now.

My tech stack for running this solo:

6 AM. Recording content. Coffee brewing. This is the life.
What my day actually looks like:
The boring stuff nobody sees:
Behind the camera:
Current setup for content creation:
Building a fitness business in 2026. What would you want to know?
If you could automate one thing in your business, what would it be?
What's stopping you from starting?
Solo founders: what's your biggest challenge right now?
Peptide education + personal training. Weird combo? Or perfect?

reply_triggers: What would you want to see more of?
bookmark_triggers: Save this for when you're ready to start
Reference this next time you're stuck
retweet_triggers: Share this with someone building in public

examples:
Week 3 revenue: $847

- 2 coaching clients: $600
- YouTube ad revenue: $47
- Affiliate link: $200

Not quitting my day job yet.
But it's growing.

What's your Week 3 goal?

Monthly recap: February 2026

Revenue: $3,240
Costs: $890 (tools, ads, content)
Profit: $2,350

Margin: 72%

Not life-changing money yet.
But 100% better than $0.

Following along? Reply with your numbers.

First $1,000 month.

Took 4 months of:
- Daily content
- 200+ DMs
- 3 failed strategies

Most gave up by month 2.

What's your timeline for first $1K?

Just hit 1,000 X followers.

90 days ago: 47 followers.

What changed:
- Posted 3x/day minimum
- Replied to everyone
- Focused on providing value first

Small milestone. Feels big.

What's your next milestone?

First coaching client from X.

$300/month recurring.

Came from a thread about BPC-157.
They asked a question.
I answered in detail.
They DM'd.

Content → Trust → Revenue.

Anyone else getting clients from X?

YouTube hit 500 subscribers.

6 months of weekly uploads.
Most videos got <100 views.
3 videos carried everything.

Consistency > virality.

What platform are you focusing on?

Spent $500 on ads. Made $47.

Lesson: I don't know ads (yet).

Going back to organic for now.
Will learn ads when I have more budget to burn.

Anyone else waste money learning the hard way?

Lost a $400/month client this week.

Reason: I was too slow to respond.

New rule: 2-hour max response time.

Simple fix. Expensive lesson.

What's a lesson that cost you money?

Thought I could do everything.

- Film content
- Edit videos
- Write posts
- Run coaching
- Build automation

Result: Nothing done well.

Hiring an editor next month.

What's the first thing you outsourced?

My solo fitness business tech stack:

- n8n for automation
- Claude for content ideas
- Google Sheets for tracking
- Notion for client management
- Grok for image generation

Total cost: ~$50/month.

What's in your stack?

Just automated my X posting.

n8n workflow:
1. Reads from Google Sheets
2. Posts at optimal times
3. Updates status
4. Tracks analytics

Now I batch content on Sunday.
Posts itself all week.

What have you automated?

AI is handling:
- Content research
- First drafts
- Image creation
- Email responses

I'm handling:
- Final edits
- Strategy
- Client calls
- Being human

The hybrid approach works.

How are you using AI?

What my day actually looks like:

5:00 AM - Wake up, gym
7:00 AM - Content batch (1 hour)
8:00 AM - Day job starts
5:00 PM - Day job ends
6:00 PM - Client calls
8:00 PM - Family time
10:00 PM - Planning tomorrow

No magic. Just systems.

What's your schedule?

Recording setup for under $200:

- iPhone 13 (already had)
- $30 tripod from Amazon
- Ring light: $40
- Lav mic: $25
- Free editing software

You don't need expensive gear.
You need to start.

What's holding you back?

The boring stuff nobody sees:

- 2 hours tracking metrics
- Responding to 50+ DMs
- Uploading/scheduling content
- Fixing broken automations
- Researching new studies

The glamour is 10%.
The grind is 90%.

Who else is in the grind phase?

Building a fitness business while working full-time.

Asking everyone who's done this:

When did you quit your job?

Or did you keep both?

Peptide education + personal training.

Seems like a weird combo to some.

To me, it's both about optimization.

What's your niche combo?

If you could automate one thing in your business tomorrow, what would it be?

I'll share how I'd do it.

Reply below.

Week 12 goals:

- Finish 3 YouTube videos
- Land 1 new coaching client
- Hit 1,500 X followers
- Automate client onboarding

Public accountability.

What are your goals this week?

This week I'm focusing on:

1. Peptide safety framework video
2. Client check-ins
3. Testing new content format

Simplifying.

Less is more.

What's your ONE focus this week?

Week 11 recap:

Wins:
- +127 X followers
- $600 in coaching revenue
- Finished content calendar

Losses:
- YouTube video flopped (42 views)
- Missed 2 client check-ins

Next week: Fix the systems.

How was your week?

Friday recap.

Best: Landed my 5th coaching client
Worst: Broke my posting automation (again)
Learned: Need better error handling

Overall: Good week.

Drop your wins below.

monday.description: Set the tone for the week, share goals

tuesday.description: Show the work behind the results

wednesday.description: Celebrate progress, no matter how small

hashtag_strategy.usage: Max 2-3 hashtags per post, focused on #buildinpublic community
